input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

### 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store | Required for iOS SDK, simulators, and build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Installs compilers & CLI utilities |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Use the macOS installer; includes the `dotnet` CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | `dotnet workload install maui` | Installs all MAUI platform packs |
| **Android Studio** *(optional but recommended)* | [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) | Easiest way to manage Android SDK, emulators & JDK |

### 2. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See available .NET SDKs & recommended versions
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet workload search          # lists installable workloads
dotnet workload list            # shows what's installed

# Check Xcode version (must match Apple's min for your iOS target)
xcodebuild -version

# Check Android SDK (if installed via Android Studio)
~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list
```

The [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) document exact Xcode/Android SDK version requirements per .NET release.

### 3. Post-Install Configuration

```bash
# Accept Xcode license
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# Accept Android SDK licenses
~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --licenses

# Set ANDROID_HOME if not already set
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
```

### 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create & build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android Emulator (start one in Android Studio first)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

Replace `net9.0` with your actual TFM (`net8.0`, etc.).

### 5. Recommended IDE

- **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) extension — free, lightweight
- **JetBrains Rider** — full-featured, paid

### Quick Checklist

1. ✅ Xcode installed + license accepted
2. ✅ .NET SDK installed (`dotnet --version`)
3. ✅ MAUI workload installed (`dotnet workload list` shows `maui`)
4. ✅ Android SDK + licenses accepted, `ANDROID_HOME` set
5. ✅ `dotnet new maui` project builds for both `-ios` and `-android`
